question_title: What will be the value of "ICE BofA BB US High Yield Index Effective Yield" on 2026-03-10?
summary: The ICE BofA BB US High Yield Index Effective Yield was 5.39% on Feb 26, 2026, and has been remarkably stable in the 5.36-5.40 range over the past week. This stability reflects offsetting forces: declining Treasury yields (10Y fell from 4.29 to 4.02 in February) pulling BB yields down, while slightly widening BB credit spreads (1.75->1.79) push yields up. Over the 8 trading days to March 10, a Monte Carlo bootstrap simulation using empirical daily changes from the Sep 2025-Feb 2026 stable period produces a distribution centered near 5.38 with a standard deviation of ~0.14. The March 6 jobs report is the key catalyst that could cause a meaningful move. The distribution is approximately symmetric around the current level with near-zero drift.

factors.description: Current value anchoring: BB yield at 5.39 on Feb 26, very stable at 5.36-5.40 range over last 7 trading days
Empirical daily volatility of ~0.05 over recent months suggests 8-day standard deviation of ~0.14, giving a roughly symmetric distribution centered near current value
Mild negative drift in full stable period (-0.0015/day) but essentially flat in last 30 days (+0.0003/day) - near zero drift expected
BB OAS spread slightly widening (1.75->1.79 in last week), suggesting modest upward pressure on BB yields from credit risk repricing
Treasury yields declining (10Y: 4.29->4.02 in Feb), partially offsetting spread widening and keeping BB yield stable/slightly lower
Jobs report on March 6 is a key catalyst that could move yields in either direction; historically can cause 0.05-0.15 yield moves
Fed funds rate futures imply continued gradual easing (3.64->3.58 May, 3.47 Jul), supportive of lower overall yield environment but already priced in

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

The ICE BofA BB US High Yield Index Effective Yield on March 10, 2026 is forecast to remain near its current level of 5.39%, with moderate uncertainty driven by the ~8 trading day horizon and an intervening jobs report on March 6.

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 5.20% |
| P20 | 5.26% |
| P40 | 5.34% |
| P60 | 5.41% |
| P80 | 5.50% |
| P90 | 5.56% |

**Key drivers:**
- The yield has been extremely stable (5.36-5.40) over the past week, with offsetting forces: declining Treasury yields vs. slightly widening credit spreads
- Empirical bootstrap simulation from the Sep 2025-Feb 2026 stable regime gives ~0.14 standard deviation over 8 trading days
- The March 6 jobs report is the primary catalyst that could cause a meaningful directional move
- Near-zero drift means the distribution is centered very close to the current 5.39%
